fact: December 24 falls exactly 2 days after the winter solstice in the Northern Hemisphere, which means the days are already getting longer again - but only by about 1 minute!
In Antarctica, December 24 has nearly 24 hours of blazing sunlight because it's summer down there - penguins are having beach parties while it's dark up north!
In cold regions, house cats typically sleep up to 20 hours a day during late December to conserve energy!
Tree sap in maple trees moves so slowly on December 24 in cold climates that it's almost completely stopped - like the trees are holding their breath until spring!
The sun sets before 5 PM in most of the northern United States on December 24, making it one of the earliest sunset days of the year!
Reindeer in the Arctic have special eyes that change color from gold in summer to blue in winter - and by December 24, they're at their bluest!
In Australia and New Zealand, December 24 temperatures can hit 100°F (38°C) - hot enough to fry an egg on the sidewalk while people in Canada are bundled in snow gear!
The zodiac sign Capricorn begins on December 22, so December 24 is when this earth sign is just getting started!
Hibernating bears have been asleep for about a month by December 24, and their heart rate has slowed down to just 8 beats per minute - that's slower than a sleeping human!
December 24 is exactly 7 days before New Year's Eve, making it the perfect day for nature to start its final countdown to a brand new year!
Evergreen trees like pine and spruce are at their most fragrant on cold December days because the chilly air makes their oils more concentrated - it's like nature's own air freshener!
In the Southern Hemisphere, December 24 marks the peak of summer, so flowers are blooming everywhere while Northern Hemisphere plants are taking their winter nap!
